evals.prompt:
Read the existing Drupal module at /tmp/phase15-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/ to understand its current code. This module has Project and Task content entities with CRUD routes, entity forms, a settings form, dashboard controller, and basic permissions.

Extend this module with the following:

1. **Entity Access Control Handlers**: Create `ProjectAccessControlHandler` and `TaskAccessControlHandler` classes extending `EntityAccessControlHandler`. Each handler should implement `checkAccess()` with per-operation logic (view, update, delete) and `checkCreateAccess()`. For update/delete, check ownership (entity owner can edit/delete own) OR admin permission. Register these handlers in the entity annotations.

2. **Granular Permissions**: Expand permissions to include per-entity CRUD operations: create project, edit own project, edit any project, delete own project, delete any project, view project (and the same set for tasks). Use `permission_callbacks` in the permissions YAML to generate these dynamically from a PHP class rather than hardcoding 12+ permission entries.

3. **Project Status Block Plugin**: Create a block plugin at `src/Plugin/Block/ProjectStatusBlock.php` that displays project counts grouped by status. It needs the entity type manager service injected via dependency injection. Add a `blockAccess()` method to restrict the block to users with the dashboard permission.

4. **AI Agent Tools**: Create two AiFunctionCall plugin classes for the AI Agents module:
   - `src/Plugin/AiFunctionCall/CreateProjectTool.php` -- creates a project entity with title, description, and status
   - `src/Plugin/AiFunctionCall/QueryProjectsTool.php` -- queries projects with optional status filter
   Each plugin should implement `execute()` and `getArguments()` methods with proper plugin-style dependency injection.

5. **AI Optional Dependency**: Ensure the module can still be installed and function without the AI module. AI-related plugin classes should only load when the AI module is present. Update .info.yml if needed.

6. **State-changing action route**: Add a route at `/admin/content/project/{project}/complete` that marks a project as completed. This is a non-form action link, so it needs CSRF protection. Add the route, controller method, and link generation.

Create all new files and modify existing files in /tmp/phase15-VARIANT/web/modules/custom/group_ai_pm/
After creating/modifying files, run: cd /tmp/phase15-VARIANT && ddev drush cr 2>/dev/null; true
Do NOT ask questions -- just create the code.
evals.expected_output: Extended module with EntityAccessControlHandlers, dynamic permissions, a block plugin with DI, AiFunctionCall plugins with plugin DI, optional AI dependency handling, and CSRF-protected action route.
expectations: EntityAccessControlHandler class exists for Project or Task that extends EntityAccessControlHandler and implements checkAccess() with a switch or match on $operation for at least 'view', 'update', and 'delete' operations (NOT just relying on the admin_permission annotation key -- the entity MUST reference a custom access handler class in its handlers array)

At least one AccessResult in an access handler uses ->addCacheContexts() or ->addCacheTags() (bare AccessResult::allowed() or ::forbidden() without cache metadata causes the first user's access decision to be cached and applied to ALL subsequent users -- AccessResult::allowedIfHasPermission() is also acceptable as it adds cache contexts automatically)

permissions.yml uses `permission_callbacks` key pointing to a PHP class method that generates per-entity CRUD permissions dynamically (NOT hardcoding all 12+ permissions as static YAML entries -- permission_callbacks is the Drupal pattern for generating permissions programmatically from entity types or bundles)

At least one plugin class (Block, AiFunctionCall, or other) implements ContainerFactoryPluginInterface AND has a create() method with the 4-parameter signature: create(ContainerInterface $container, array $configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) -- NOT the controller 1-parameter signature create(ContainerInterface $container) which causes a fatal error when the plugin manager passes 4 arguments

At least one plugin constructor calls parent::__construct($configuration, $plugin_id, $plugin_definition) before storing injected services (without this call, the plugin loses its configuration array, plugin ID, and definition metadata -- block placement settings and plugin inspection methods break silently)

Block plugin has a blockAccess() method that returns an AccessResult (restricting block visibility based on permissions -- without blockAccess(), the block is visible to everyone including anonymous users regardless of what content it displays)

New admin/dangerous permissions (like 'delete any project' or 'administer group_ai_pm') use `restrict access: true` in permissions.yml or the permission_callbacks return array (this flag shows a security warning on the admin permissions page -- without it, site builders may grant dangerous permissions without realizing the risk)

A route for the state-changing action (mark complete or similar) includes `_csrf_token: 'TRUE'` in its requirements section (non-form action links that change state MUST have CSRF protection -- without it, an attacker can craft a URL that changes entity state when clicked by an authenticated user)

NO static \Drupal:: service calls in any NEW plugin class files under src/Plugin/ (plugin classes that need services MUST inject them via ContainerFactoryPluginInterface create() + constructor -- \Drupal:: calls bypass DI, making plugins untestable)

Entity annotation/attribute handlers array for at least one entity includes an 'access' key pointing to the custom access handler class (NOT just using admin_permission -- admin_permission only provides a global on/off check; custom access handlers enable per-operation, per-user, ownership-based access control)
